hero.tagline: Create, share and play your chord sheets.
hero.subtitle: The tool that was missing to organize your repertoire, rehearse and get on stage.

book.title: My book of chord sheets.
book.quote: We know. You've learned dozens of songs. But when it's time to play — the exact chords, the key, the sequence — it's not quite at hand anymore.
book.quoteFooter: That's exactly why the book exists.
book.body: Your book is your personal library. Add your own creations, but also community sheets you're interested in. Your whole repertoire, always within reach — for rehearsing, improvising or getting on stage.
book.item1: Add any public sheet in one click
book.item2: Find your whole repertoire in one place
book.item3: Organize into setlists for every gig
book.item4: Transpose, play, print — without leaving your book

bands.title: Play together.
bands.subtitle: Create a band, invite by link. Share your sheets with every member instantly — whether they're on stage or in class.
bands.musicText: The whole band plays the same version. Share a common repertoire, rehearse with the same chords, get on stage in sync.
bands.musicItem1: One shared repertoire for every member
bands.musicItem2: Everyone gets the sheets in their own book
bands.musicItem4: Synced concert mode to play together
bands.teachersText: You create a sheet, the whole class gets it in their book. By class, by level, by instrument — no more printing or links lost in emails.
bands.teachersItem1: Distribute a sheet to a whole class in one click
bands.teachersItem2: Organize your students into separate groups
bands.teachersItem3: Every student has access without searching
bands.teachersItem4: Students can transpose and play on their own
bands.pricingText: One subscription for the whole band.
bands.pricingSubtext: Teacher, students, musicians — one shared license, not one per head.

features.title: Everything you need to play.
features.subtitle: From creation to the stage, without friction.

editor.title: Visual editor
editor.text: Build your sheet in minutes. Import from Ultimate Guitar or enter it cell by cell.

concert.title: Concert mode
concert.text: Full-screen auto-scroll, adjustable BPM. Play without ever looking away.

transpose.title: Transposition
transpose.text: Change key in one click. Perfect for adapting to the singer or changing capo.

instruments.title: Every instrument
instruments.text: Guitar, piano, ukulele, bass, mandolin — chord diagrams generated automatically.

sharing.title: Sharing & setlists
sharing.text: Publish your sheets, share by link, organize your sets for gigs.

print.title: Clean printing
print.text: Optimized A4 layout with diagrams, repeats and sections — ready in one click.

ai.title: AI recognition
ai.text: Snap a photo of sheet music or a paper chord chart — AI recognizes the chords and builds your sheet automatically.

dictionary.title: Chord dictionary
dictionary.text: Full library for guitar, piano, ukulele and more. Identify a chord by placing your fingers on the fretboard or keyboard.

how.title: Simple from the start.
how.subtitle: Three steps from zero to the stage.

step1.title: Create or import
step1.text: Enter it visually, paste chords from Ultimate Guitar, or snap a photo of sheet music — AI handles the rest.

step2.title: Organize and share
step2.text: Store it in your book, build setlists, transpose. Share with your band or hand it to a whole class in one click.

step3.title: Get on stage
step3.text: Turn on concert mode: full screen, BPM-synced scroll, drum machine. Keep the whole band in sync in real time.

finalCta.title: Ready to organize
finalCta.subtitle: Free, no credit card. Your repertoire online in 2 minutes.

WelcomeBanner.title: What exactly is this?
WelcomeBanner.body1: Not a music course — more like a logbook for musicians. Jot down the chords and structure of a song (Intro, Verse, Chorus…) for a simplified view and play without searching.
WelcomeBanner.body2: Build your personal book, find your songs at a glance, and share them with other musicians. A chord sheet doesn't replace the score — it reminds you of the essentials.
WelcomeBanner.pillar1Desc: All your songs in one place

LegalMentions.ipBody: All elements making up this site (design, logo, text, code) are the exclusive property of Alexandre Vauthier, unless otherwise stated. Any reproduction, representation, or distribution, in whole or in part, on any medium whatsoever, without express authorization, is prohibited and would constitute an infringement punishable under the French Intellectual Property Code.
LegalMentions.liabilityBody: Alexandre Vauthier strives to ensure the accuracy of the information on the site but cannot guarantee its completeness or currency. The publisher reserves the right to correct or modify the content at any time without notice.
LegalMentions.lawBody: This site is governed by French law. Any dispute relating to its use will be submitted to the competent courts of Rouen, France.

LegalCgu.s1Body: These Terms of Use ("ToU") govern access to and use of the ChordSheet platform, available at chordsheet.app, published by Alexandre Vauthier (sole proprietor, 195 rue Beauvoisine, 76000 Rouen, France). By creating an account, the user accepts these Terms of Use without reservation.
LegalCgu.s2Body: ChordSheet is accessible to any individual who creates an account via email and password. Registration is free. A paid premium offer may be introduced later; its terms will be specified in the applicable Terms of Sale.
LegalCgu.s3Body: The user is responsible for the confidentiality of their account and for any activity carried out from it. They agree not to create an account with false information and to immediately notify the publisher of any unauthorized access to their account.
LegalCgu.s4Title: 4. User content and assignment of rights
LegalCgu.s4Body1: The user retains ownership of the chord sheets they create. By publishing a sheet in public mode, the user grants Alexandre Vauthier a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, sublicensable, and transferable license to use, reproduce, distribute, display, and adapt this content for the purpose of operating and promoting ChordSheet.
LegalCgu.s4Body2: This license ends when the user deletes the content or switches the sheet back to private mode, subject to technical processing delays. Private sheets are only accessible to their author.
LegalCgu.s5Intro: It is in particular prohibited to:
LegalCgu.s5Item1: publish unlawful, defamatory, offensive, or infringing content;
LegalCgu.s5Item2: fully reproduce copyrighted song lyrics without authorization;
LegalCgu.s5Item3: attempt to bypass the service's security measures;
LegalCgu.s5Item4: use the service for unauthorized commercial purposes.
LegalCgu.s6Body: ChordSheet, its interface, code, and proprietary content are protected by intellectual property law. Any reproduction without written authorization is prohibited.
LegalCgu.s7Body: The publisher cannot be held liable for content published by users or for indirect damages resulting from use of the service. The service is provided "as is" and may be interrupted at any time for maintenance or technical reasons.
LegalCgu.s8Body: The publisher reserves the right to modify these Terms of Use at any time. The user will be informed by email or notification on the service. Continued use of the service after a change constitutes acceptance of the new Terms of Use. The publisher may suspend or delete an account in the event of non-compliance with these terms.
LegalCgu.s9Intro: Chord sheets (chords and rhythm) published on ChordSheet are not, in themselves, considered infringing. However, if content published by a user (fully reproduced lyrics, title, artwork, or any other element) infringes a copyright, related right, or any other intellectual property right, the rights holder may report it to <email>alex.vauthier@gmail.com</email>, specifying:
LegalCgu.s9Item1: identification of the content concerned (link to the sheet);
LegalCgu.s9Item2: the right invoked and the capacity of the requester (author, publisher, rights holder…);
LegalCgu.s9Body2: In accordance with article 6-I-2 et seq. of French law no. 2004-575 of June 21, 2004 on confidence in the digital economy (LCEN), the publisher undertakes to promptly remove or disable access to any manifestly unlawful content notified under these conditions. An account that is the source of repeated and substantiated reports may be suspended or deleted.
LegalCgu.s10Body: These Terms of Use are governed by French law. Any dispute will be submitted to the competent courts of Rouen, France.

LegalCgv.s1Body: ChordSheet is published by Alexandre Vauthier, sole proprietor, residing at 195 rue Beauvoisine, 76000 Rouen, France.
LegalCgv.s2Body: These Terms of Sale ("ToS") govern the purchase of the ChordSheet Pro subscription, which grants access to advanced features of the ChordSheet web app (chordsheet.app).
LegalCgv.s3Intro: The ChordSheet Pro subscription is offered at the following rates:
LegalCgv.s3Body1: Prices are shown in euros, all taxes included. Alexandre Vauthier is a sole proprietor not subject to VAT (article 293 B of the French Tax Code) — the prices shown are therefore final prices.
LegalCgv.s3Body2: Rates may change at any time. Active subscriptions are not affected by a price increase before their renewal.
LegalCgv.s4Item3: Multi-sheet PDF export of an entire Set (setlist) into a single document
LegalCgv.s4Item4: Unlimited OCR sheet analyses (fair use: 100 analyses/month)
LegalCgv.s4Body: The free plan's features (unlimited sheets, PDF export, custom chords, 2 OCR analyses/month) remain accessible without a subscription.
LegalCgv.s5Body1: The subscription is purchased online via the ChordSheet pricing page. Payment is processed by Stripe (Stripe Payments Europe, Ltd), a secure payment provider. Bank details are not stored by ChordSheet.
LegalCgv.s5Body2: The subscription renews automatically at each billing cycle (monthly or yearly) until cancelled. A receipt is issued by Stripe at each renewal.
LegalCgv.s6Body1: In accordance with article L.221-28 of the French Consumer Code, the 14-day right of withdrawal does not apply to digital content whose performance began with your express consent before the withdrawal period expired.
LegalCgv.s6Body2: However, if you have not used the Pro features since subscribing, you can request a refund within 14 days of purchase by contacting <email>alex.vauthier@gmail.com</email>.
LegalCgv.s7Body: You can cancel your subscription at any time from your profile ("My subscription" section). Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period — no prorated refund is issued. Your data and sheets remain accessible after cancellation.
LegalCgv.s8Body: ChordSheet is provided "as is". While we do our best to ensure continuity of service, we do not guarantee uninterrupted availability. In the event of a prolonged interruption attributable to ChordSheet, a prorated refund may be granted upon request.
LegalCgv.s9Body1: These Terms of Sale are governed by French law. In the event of a dispute, an amicable solution will be sought first. Failing that, the competent courts of Rouen, France will have jurisdiction.
LegalCgv.s9Body2: In accordance with article L.616-1 of the French Consumer Code, you may use the free consumer mediation service. The competent mediator is the European online dispute resolution platform:

LegalConfidentialite.effectiveDate: Effective as of April 21, 2025 — GDPR compliant
LegalConfidentialite.s1Intro: The controller for personal data processing is:
LegalConfidentialite.s2Intro: When using ChordSheet, the following data is collected:
LegalConfidentialite.s3Body: Data is hosted on Google Firebase servers (Google Ireland Limited, Dublin, Ireland). Google may transfer data to servers located outside the European Union, under standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission.
LegalConfidentialite.s4Body: Data is retained for as long as the account is active, then deleted within 30 days of the account closure request. Sheets published in public mode may be retained in anonymized form for statistical purposes.
LegalConfidentialite.s5Body: ChordSheet uses technical cookies strictly necessary for authentication to function (Firebase Authentication). No advertising or third-party tracking cookies are set.
LegalConfidentialite.s6Intro: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights over your personal data:
LegalConfidentialite.s6Item1: Access: obtain a copy of your data;
LegalConfidentialite.s6Item4: Portability: receive your data in a structured format;
LegalConfidentialite.s6Item5: Objection: object to processing based on legitimate interest.
LegalConfidentialite.s6Body: To exercise these rights, contact us at alex.vauthier@gmail.com. If a complaint is unresolved, you can contact the CNIL (www.cnil.fr), the French data protection authority.
LegalConfidentialite.s7Body: This policy may be updated. Any substantial change will be notified by email to affected users.

Credits.intro: A tool is never born alone. Here are the people who, closely or from a distance, had a hand in making it.
Credits.familyBody: It all starts there. Julien — my brother, Piza — whose musical ear and sense of theory are in every measure of this tool. My father, who put guitars in our hands before we knew what to do with them. My mother, who has since picked up guitar and bass herself — because once music gets into a house, it never really leaves.
Credits.bandBody: The people it all really started with — the first rehearsals in garages and at the youth club, the first gigs, the first riffs learned together. ChordSheet exists a little bit for them, and because of them.
Credits.gregBody: A brilliant guitarist and a man of vision. Greg was one of the first to understand what this tool could become, to help shape it with me, and to ask the right questions at the right time. The kind of outside perspective that changes everything.
Credits.bastienBody: A guitarist and a developer — the dream combination for this project. Bastien understands both sides of it: what a musician expects from a tool, and how to build it properly. His help means a lot.
Credits.closing: And to everyone who uses ChordSheet, shares their chord sheets, and sends feedback — thank you. You're the best reason to keep going.

Faq.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to get started with ChordSheet.
Faq.s1a1: ChordSheet is a web app for creating, organizing, and sharing chord sheets. You can enter your sheets by hand, import them from chord text, or extract them automatically from a photo of sheet music. Sheets are accessible from any device, and you can print them or use them live on stage without installing anything.
Faq.s1q2: How do I create a sheet?
Faq.s1a2: From the dashboard or the "+ Sheet" button at the top right, you have three options: start from a blank sheet and enter chords by hand, paste text in chord+lyrics format, or drop in a photo of sheet music for automatic AI transcription.
Faq.s2q1: How do I import a sheet from text?
Faq.s2a1: Copy the text of a sheet in chord+lyrics format (chords written above the lyrics, sections named like [Verse] or [Chorus]), then in ChordSheet click "+ Sheet" → "Import text" and paste it in. The app automatically detects the title, artist, key, tempo, and section structure.
Faq.s2q2: How does sheet music analysis work?
Faq.s2a2: You upload one or more photos of your sheet music (JPG, PNG, WebP). An AI model analyzes the images, identifies chords measure by measure, the song structure (intro, verse, chorus…), repeats, and the time signature, then automatically generates a sheet. You can then correct it in the editor.
Faq.s2q3: The detected chords are wrong, what should I do?
Faq.s2a3: Text import and sheet music analysis are automatic interpretations — the AI does its best but can make mistakes, especially with durations, complex chords, or ambiguous passages. That's completely normal. In the editor, you can correct or override any chord however you like: change the name, extend or split the duration, add or remove cells. The sheet is yours.
Faq.s3a1: Yes. In the editor, you can define your own fingerings for chords that aren't in the library, or modify an existing fingering. These custom chords are saved with the sheet and shown in the diagrams.
Faq.s3a2: ChordSheet supports seven instruments: Guitar, Ukulele, Mandolin, Banjo, Bass, Piano, and Voice. Each instrument has its own chord library and diagrams (fretboard or keyboard depending on the instrument).
Faq.s3q3: Is French notation (Do Ré Mi) supported?
Faq.s3a3: Yes. In your profile, you can choose between American notation (C D E F G A B) and French notation (Do Ré Mi Fa Sol La Si). The display switches across the whole app, while chords remain stored in American notation.
Faq.s4q1: Are my sheets visible to everyone?
Faq.s4a1: No, all sheets are private by default. You can make them public from the editor (they then appear on the Explore page), or share them via a unique link without making them public ("unlisted" mode).
Faq.s4a2: A Group lets you share sheets with other musicians. The creator invites members via a link, and sheets linked to the group are accessible to all members, even if they're private. Handy for a band or a class.
Faq.s4a3: A Set is a setlist: an ordered list of sheets for a gig or rehearsal. In concert mode ("Play"), sheets scroll through in full screen, chords are enlarged, and you move from one song to the next with a swipe or a click.
Faq.s4q4: What's the difference between a Set and a Group?
Faq.s4a4: A Set is a personal planning tool: you put songs in order for a gig or rehearsal, and play them in full-screen mode. A Group is a shared space: it brings musicians together and gives them access to the same sheets. The two are complementary — you can build a Set from sheets shared in your Group.
Faq.s5q1: How do I transpose a sheet?
Faq.s5a1: In viewing mode, a transpose selector is available at the bottom of the screen. You can shift from −6 to +6 semitones in real time. Transposition is just a display setting: the original sheet is kept as is.
Faq.s5a2: Yes. A print button is available in viewing mode. The layout adapts automatically for a clean printout: the navbar and controls disappear, and chord diagrams can optionally be included.
